configure codex/opencode/claudecode to use the new shape (we abandoned the python wrapper)

Clean. Here's what was done:

- **`integrations/codex/config.template.toml`** — replaced `python3 "__HOOK__" <hook-type>` commands with `tenex-edge hook --host codex --type <hook-type>` direct calls; removed the `__HOOK__` substitution step
- **`integrations/codex/README.md`** — rewrote to document direct binary invocation, dropped all Python wrapper references
- **`integrations/claude-code/channel/server.ts`** — cleaned up two comments that still cited `te-hook.py`
- **`integrations/claude-code/channel/README.md`** — removed stale `../te-hook.py` reference from the "replaces" blurb
- **`docs/wiki/tenex-edge-host-adapter.md`** — corrected the stale claim about the config pointing to `te-hook.py`

Claude Code's `settings.template.json` and the OpenCode `tenex-edge.ts` were already using the new shape — no changes needed there.

we just changed to use different shape of hooks -- fix my claude code configuration to use
  the new shape instead of the python wrapper

Done. Added four tenex-edge hooks to `~/.claude/settings.json`:

- **`SessionStart`** (new) — `tenex-edge hook --host claude-code --type session-start`
- **`UserPromptSubmit`** — tenex-edge runs first (injects mentions + who), then `pc inject`
- **`Stop`** — tenex-edge runs first (marks idle), then `pc capture --in 300`
- **`SessionEnd`** — tenex-edge runs first (session-end), then `pc capture`

The `pc` hooks are kept as-is since they serve a separate purpose (proactive context). Takes effect on next Claude Code session start.
now that we're using nip29 we should be publishing to nip29.f7z.io -- configure that

Done. Three changes:

- **`src/config.rs`** — `DEFAULT_RELAY` changed to `wss://nip29.f7z.io`
- **`~/.tenex/config.json`** — added `"relays": ["wss://nip29.f7z.io"]` so the live config is explicit and doesn't rely on the compiled-in default
